reason: NOTATION ABUSE ADEQUATE BUT WORTH FLAGGING: The proof writes p^alpha(tau_*(t)) = p^alpha(t) (line 264-265 of lecA1.tex). This reparametrizes p^alpha from a function of proper time tau to a function of coordinate time t. The abuse of notation is explicitly flagged in the text ('Writing ... as functions of coordinate time'), which is good practice. Similarly, (d gamma^beta/dt)(tau) in Step 1 is somewhat ambiguous: d gamma^beta/dt is naturally a function of t, but the notation suggests evaluation at tau. What is meant is (d gamma^beta/dt)|_{t=f(tau)}. The text does not clarify this. These are standard physics conventions but in a proof claiming distributional rigor, the notation should be more careful.
